  Manages a WellPlayed organization identity provider (IdP) for OAuth2 or OpenID Connect authentication. Backed by the createIdentityProvider, updateIdentityProvider, and deleteIdentityProvider mutations.

# wellplayed_identity_provider (Resource)

Manages a WellPlayed organization identity provider (IdP) for OAuth2 or OpenID Connect authentication. Backed by the `createIdentityProvider`, `updateIdentityProvider`, and `deleteIdentityProvider` mutations.

Provide at most one of `oauth2_configuration` or `openid_configuration`. A provider can also be derived from a root identity provider via `identity_provider_id` instead of an inline configuration.

The configuration blocks carry the `client_secret` and are returned by the API only as an opaque union, so they are managed write-only from configuration: sent on create/update but not refreshed during Read, and out-of-band changes are not detected as drift.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.

# A minimal OAuth2 identity provider.
resource "wellplayed_identity_provider" "oauth2" {
  name                           = "Acme SSO"
  description                    = "Acme corporate single sign-on."
  enabled                        = true
  required_for_player_validation = false
  allow_login                    = true

  oauth2_configuration = {
    provider_type = "OAUTH2"
    client_id     = "acme-client-id"
    client_secret = var.acme_client_secret
    redirect_url  = "https://play.example.com/auth/callback"

    authorization_url          = "https://sso.acme.com/oauth2/authorize"
    token_endpoint             = "https://sso.acme.com/oauth2/token"
    token_endpoint_auth_method = "CLIENT_SECRET_POST"

    # Fetch the user profile after authentication and map fields onto the
    # player profile.
    data_retrievers = [
      {
        url = "https://sso.acme.com/oauth2/userinfo"

        headers = [
          {
            name  = "Accept"
            value = "application/json"
          }
        ]

        mappings = [
          {
            path      = "email"
            mapped_to = "email"
          },
          {
            path      = "sub"
            mapped_to = "external_id"
            private   = true
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}

# An OpenID Connect provider derived from its issuer.
resource "wellplayed_identity_provider" "openid" {
  name                           = "Acme OIDC"
  description                    = "Acme OpenID Connect."
  enabled                        = true
  required_for_player_validation = true
  allow_login                    = true

  openid_configuration = {
    provider_type = "OPENID"
    client_id     = "acme-oidc-client"
    client_secret = var.acme_oidc_secret
    redirect_url  = "https://play.example.com/auth/callback"
    issuer        = "https://sso.acme.com"

    data_retrievers = []
  }
}

variable "acme_client_secret" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}

variable "acme_oidc_secret" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `allow_login` (Boolean) Whether users can log in using this identity provider.
- `description` (String) Human-readable description of the identity provider.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether this identity provider is currently active.
- `name` (String) Display name of the identity provider.
- `required_for_player_validation` (Boolean) Whether player accounts must be validated through this provider.

### Optional

- `icon` (String) URL or identifier for the provider icon.
- `identity_provider_id` (String) ID of the root identity provider this provider is derived from, if any. Changing it forces a new provider.
- `oauth2_configuration` (Attributes) OAuth2 client configuration. Mutually exclusive with `openid_configuration`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--oauth2_configuration))
- `openid_configuration` (Attributes) OpenID Connect configuration. Mutually exclusive with `oauth2_configuration`. The OAuth2-only fields (`token_endpoint`, `authorization_url`, `link_redirect_url`, `token_endpoint_auth_method`) are ignored here. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--openid_configuration))

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) When the identity provider was created (RFC 3339).
- `id` (String) Unique identifier of the identity provider.
- `organization_id` (String) ID of the organization this provider belongs to.
- `updated_at` (String) When the identity provider was last updated (RFC 3339).

<a id="nestedatt--oauth2_configuration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `oauth2_configuration`

Required:

- `client_id` (String) OAuth2 client ID issued by the identity provider.
- `client_secret` (String, Sensitive) OAuth2 client secret issued by the identity provider.
- `provider_type` (String) Identity provider protocol. One of `OPENID`, `OAUTH2`.
- `redirect_url` (String) URL to redirect users back to after authentication.

Optional:

- `authorization_endpoint` (String) OAuth2/OIDC authorization endpoint URL.
- `authorization_url` (String) URL of the OAuth2 authorization endpoint where users are redirected to authenticate. OAuth2 only.
- `data_retrievers` (Attributes List) Endpoints to retrieve user data from after authentication. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--oauth2_configuration--data_retrievers))
- `issuer` (String) Issuer identifier for the identity provider.
- `link_redirect_url` (String) URL users are redirected to after the identity linking process completes. Required for the IdP-based identity linking flow via `generateIdentityLinkUrl`. OAuth2 only.
- `token_endpoint` (String) URL of the OAuth2 token endpoint for exchanging authorization codes. OAuth2 only.
- `token_endpoint_auth_method` (String) Authentication method used when calling the token endpoint. One of `CLIENT_SECRET_POST`, `CLIENT_SECRET_BASIC`, `CLIENT_SECRET_JWT`, `PRIVATE_KEY_JWT`, `TLS_CLIENT_AUTH`, `SELF_SIGNED_TLS_CLIENT_AUTH`, `NONE`. OAuth2 only.

<a id="nestedatt--oauth2_configuration--data_retrievers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `oauth2_configuration.data_retrievers`

Required:

- `url` (String) URL of the external endpoint to fetch user data from.

Optional:

- `headers` (Attributes List) HTTP headers to include in the data retrieval request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--oauth2_configuration--data_retrievers--headers))
- `mappings` (Attributes List) Key mappings from retrieved data fields to player profile properties. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--oauth2_configuration--data_retrievers--mappings))

<a id="nestedatt--oauth2_configuration--data_retrievers--headers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `oauth2_configuration.data_retrievers.headers`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the HTTP header.
- `value` (String, Sensitive) Value of the HTTP header.


<a id="nestedatt--oauth2_configuration--data_retrievers--mappings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `oauth2_configuration.data_retrievers.mappings`

Required:

- `mapped_to` (String) Target key name to map the value to.
- `path` (String) Dot-notation path to the source value in the retrieved object.

Optional:

- `private` (Boolean) Whether the mapped value should be treated as private.




<a id="nestedatt--openid_configuration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `openid_configuration`

Required:

- `client_id` (String) OAuth2 client ID issued by the identity provider.
- `client_secret` (String, Sensitive) OAuth2 client secret issued by the identity provider.
- `provider_type` (String) Identity provider protocol. One of `OPENID`, `OAUTH2`.
- `redirect_url` (String) URL to redirect users back to after authentication.

Optional:

- `authorization_endpoint` (String) OAuth2/OIDC authorization endpoint URL.
- `authorization_url` (String) URL of the OAuth2 authorization endpoint where users are redirected to authenticate. OAuth2 only.
- `data_retrievers` (Attributes List) Endpoints to retrieve user data from after authentication. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--openid_configuration--data_retrievers))
- `issuer` (String) Issuer identifier for the identity provider.
- `link_redirect_url` (String) URL users are redirected to after the identity linking process completes. Required for the IdP-based identity linking flow via `generateIdentityLinkUrl`. OAuth2 only.
- `token_endpoint` (String) URL of the OAuth2 token endpoint for exchanging authorization codes. OAuth2 only.
- `token_endpoint_auth_method` (String) Authentication method used when calling the token endpoint. One of `CLIENT_SECRET_POST`, `CLIENT_SECRET_BASIC`, `CLIENT_SECRET_JWT`, `PRIVATE_KEY_JWT`, `TLS_CLIENT_AUTH`, `SELF_SIGNED_TLS_CLIENT_AUTH`, `NONE`. OAuth2 only.

<a id="nestedatt--openid_configuration--data_retrievers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `openid_configuration.data_retrievers`

Required:

- `url` (String) URL of the external endpoint to fetch user data from.

Optional:

- `headers` (Attributes List) HTTP headers to include in the data retrieval request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--openid_configuration--data_retrievers--headers))
- `mappings` (Attributes List) Key mappings from retrieved data fields to player profile properties. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--openid_configuration--data_retrievers--mappings))

<a id="nestedatt--openid_configuration--data_retrievers--headers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `openid_configuration.data_retrievers.headers`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the HTTP header.
- `value` (String, Sensitive) Value of the HTTP header.


<a id="nestedatt--openid_configuration--data_retrievers--mappings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `openid_configuration.data_retrievers.mappings`

Required:

- `mapped_to` (String) Target key name to map the value to.
- `path` (String) Dot-notation path to the source value in the retrieved object.

Optional:

- `private` (Boolean) Whether the mapped value should be treated as private.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.

# Identity providers are imported by their ID. The configuration block is
# managed write-only and is not refreshed on import; re-apply to reconcile it.
terraform import wellplayed_identity_provider.oauth2 idp_01h...
```
